free_text: deuteron spectra with analyzing the different
Energy distributions of photodeuterons from Li-7
have been measured for various bremsstrahlung
end-point energies. From difference energy spectra
of the deuterons it has been established that in
the region of the Li-7 giant resonance the (g,d-0)
reaction cross section occurs intensively with
formation of the He-5 final nucleus in the ground
state, and a group of deuterons has been observed
which can be due to the reaction (g,d-1) with
formation of the final nucleus in the first excited
state. The integrated cross sections of these
reactions, on the assumption that the (g,d-1)
and 3.2(0.7) MeV*mb in the energy range eg = 16.5 -
- 30.0 MeV. In addition the emission of deuterons,
which must be due to decay of Li-7 states with
energies 25 - 30 MeV into n + d + he-4 (5(2) MeV*mb),
and also to some kind of complex disintegration of
